questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The correct statement/s about Hydrogen bonding is/are
A. Hydrogen bonding exists when \(\mathrm{H}\) is covalently bonded to the highly electro negative atom.
B. Intermolecular \(\mathrm{H}\) bonding is present in o-nitro phenol
C. Intramolecular \(\mathrm{H}\) bonding is present in HF.
D. The magnitude of \(\mathrm{H}\) bonding depends on the physical state of the compound.
E. H-bonding has powerful effect on the structure and properties of compounds
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
(A) Generally hydrogen bonding exists when $\mathrm{H}$ is covalently bonded to the highly electronegative atom like $\mathrm{F}, \mathrm{O}, \mathrm{N}$. (B) Intramolecular $\mathrm{H}$ bonding is present in
(C) Intermolecular Hydrogen bonding is present in $\mathrm{HF}$ (D) The magnitude has Hydrogen bonding in solid state is greater than liquid state. (E) Hydrogen bonding has powerfull effect on the structure \& properties of compound like melting point, boiling point, density etc.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The number of species from the following in which the central atom uses sp3 hybrid orbitals in its bonding is _________.

NH3, SO2, SiO2, BeCl2, CO2, H2O, CH4, BF3
questions.solution:
H2O has sp3 hybridisation and angular shape due to 2 lone pair of electrons.

NH3 has sp3  hybridised nitrogen atom and pyramidal shape due to 1 lone pair of electrons.

SiO2 has sp3 hybridised atoms, it is a network solid and the shape is tetrahedral around each silicon atom. 

SO2 has sp2 hybridised sulphur atom and the shape is V-shaped due to 1 lone pair of electrons.

BF3 has sp2 hybridised boron atom and the shape is trigonal planar.

BeCl2→sp

CO2→sp

CH4→sp3

Hence the answer is 4

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Arrange the bonds in order of increasing ionic character in the molecules. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>LiF</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ClF</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
The ionic character in a molecule is dependent upon the electronegativity difference between the constituting atoms. The greater the difference, the greater will be the ionic character of the molecule.

N2<SO2<ClF3<K2O<LiF

The Ionic character depends upon the difference in electronegativity (bond polarity).

N2 is a covalent molecule & the difference in electrongegativities are:
|XS – X0| = 1.0
|XCl – XF| =1.0
|XK – X0| =2.6
|XLi – XF| =3.5

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A diatomic molecule has a dipole moment of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.2</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi></math>. If the bond distance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></mover></math>, then fractional charge on each atom is _____  <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> esu. (Given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>18</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi>esu</mi><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>)
questions.solution:
Given,

 μ=1.2Dμ=q×dwhere, μ = dipole moment  q = charge d= distance

q= 1.2 D1.0A0  

∴q=1.2×10−10 esu

Hence, the charge present on each atom is approximately zero.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The number of molecules/ion/s having trigonal bipyramidal shape is …….. .

PF5,BrF5,PCl5,PtCl42-,BF3,Fe(CO)5
questions.solution:
PF5 involves sp3d hybridisation and hence has trigonal bipyramidal structure.

In PCl5  the central atom is surrounded by five bond pairs and no lone paris.
Hybridisation of central atom in PCl5 molecule is sp3d giving trigonal bipyramidal shape.

In  Fe(CO)5, oxidation state of CO is zero.

It has hybridisation =dsp3.

So it has trigonal bipyramidal geometry.

BrF5; square pyramidal

PtCl4-2; square planar

BF3; Trigonal planar.

Hence the answer is 3

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Select the compound from the following that will show intramolecular hydrogen bonding.
questions.solution:
H2O,NH3,C2H5OH⇒ Intermolecular H-Bonding

The hydrogen bonding which takes place within a molecule itself is called intramolecular hydrogen bonding.

It takes place in compounds containing two groups such that one group contains a hydrogen atom linked to an electronegative atom and the other group contains a highly electronegative atom linked to another electronegative atom of the other group.

The bond is formed between the hydrogen atoms of one group with the more electronegative atom of the other group.

So the answer is:

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
\(\begin{array}{|l|l|c|l|}
\hline & \text{List - I} & & \text{List - II} \\
\hline & \text{(Elements)} & & \text{(Properties in their respective groups)} \\
\hline \text { A. } & \mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{S} & \text { I. } & \text { Elements with highest electronegativity } \\
\hline \text { B. } & \mathrm{Ge}, \mathrm{As} & \text { II. } & \text { Elements with largest atomic size } \\
\hline \text { C. } & \text { Fr, Ra } & \text { III. } & \begin{array}{l}
\text { Elements which show properties of both } \\
\text { metals and non-metal }
\end{array} \\
\hline \text { D. } & \text { F, O } & \text { IV. } & \begin{array}{l}
\text { Elements with highest negative electron } \\
\text { gain enthalpy }
\end{array} \\
\hline
\end{array}\)
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Elements with highest electronegativity $\rightarrow \mathrm{F}, \mathrm{O}$
Elements with largest atomic size $\rightarrow \mathrm{Fr}, \mathrm{Ra}$
Elements which shows properties of both metal and non-metals i.e. metalloids $\rightarrow \mathrm{Ge}$, As
Elements with highest negative electron gain enthalpy $\rightarrow \mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{S}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List I with List II
\(\begin{array}{|l|l|c|l|}
\hline & \text{ List - I } & & \text{List - II} \\
\hline \text { A. } & \text { Melting Point }[\mathrm{K}] & \text { I. } & \mathrm{T} 1>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{A} 1>\mathrm{B} \\
\hline \text { B. } & \text { Ionic Radius }\left[\mathrm{M}^{+3} / \mathrm{pm}\right] & \text { II. } & \mathrm{B}>\mathrm{T} 1>\mathrm{A} 1 \approx \mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{In} \\
\hline \text { C. } & \Delta_{\mathrm{i}} \mathrm{H}_1\left[\mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right] & \text { III. } & \mathrm{T} 1>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{A} 1>\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{B} \\
\hline \text { D. } & \text { Atomic Radius }[\mathrm{pm}] & \text { IV. } & \mathrm{B}>\mathrm{A} 1>\mathrm{T} 1>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{Ga} \\
\hline
\end{array}\)
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Melting point : $\mathrm{B}>\mathrm{A} \ell>\mathrm{T} \ell>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{Ga}$ Ionic radius $\left(\mathrm{M}^{+3} / \mathrm{pm}\right): \mathrm{T} \ell>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{A} \ell>\mathrm{B}$ $\left(\Delta_{\mathrm{IE}} \mathrm{H}\right)_1\left[\frac{\mathrm{kJ}}{\mathrm{mol}}\right]: \mathrm{B}>\mathrm{T} \ell>\mathrm{A} \ell \approx \mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{In}$
Atomic radius (in $\mathrm{pm}$ ) : $\mathrm{T} \ell>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{A} \ell>\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{B}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The element having the highest first ionization enthalpy is
questions.solution:
The first ionisation energy is the energy needed to remove the outermost, or highest energy, electron from a neutral atom in the gas phase. 

The ionisation energy decreases from top to bottom in groups, and increases from left to right across a period. Nitrogen and carbon belong to second period elements, hence they have more ionisation energy than the other two elements.

It is highest for nitrogen because nitrogen is a more stable half-filled electronic configuration.

Al<Si<C<N; IE1 order.

Hence option C is the answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement - I: Along the period, the chemical reactivity of the element gradually increases from group 1 to group 18 .
Statement - II: The nature of oxides formed by group 1 element is basic while that of group 17 elements is acidic.
In the the light above statements, choose the most appropriate from the questions given below:
options: Both statement I and Statement II are true.
Statement I is true but Statement II is False.
Statement I is false but Statement II is true.
Both Statement I and Statement II is false.
questions.solution:
The atomic and ionic radii, as we know, generally decrease in a period from left to right. As a consequence, the ionisation enthalpies generally increase (with some exceptions) and electron gain enthalpies become more negative across a period. In other words, the ionisation enthalpy of the extreme left element for a period is the least and the electron gain enthalpy of the element on the extreme right is the highest negative (Note: noble gases having completely filled shells have rather positive electron gain enthalpy values). This results in high chemical reactivity at the two extremes and the lowest in the centre. 


Group - 1 elements form basic nature oxides.  Metallic oxides are basic in nature because they react with dilute acids to form salt and water, while group - 17 elements form acidic oxides, An oxide that exhibits the property of acids is called acidic oxide. Since they are acidic, they react with a base to form a salt. Non-metals react with oxygen to form acidic compounds of oxides which are held together by covalent bonds. Therefore, statement - II is true.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The correct sequence of electron gain enthalpy of the elements listed below is

A. Ar  B. Br  C. F  D. S

Choose the most appropriate from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Electron gain enthalpy is defined as the amount of energy released when an electron is added to an isolated gaseous atom.

Electron gain enthalpy: On moving from left to right, atomic size decreases and effective nuclear charge increases. Thus, electron gain enthalpy becomes more negative. On moving down the group, atomic size increases. Thus, electron gain enthalpy becomes less negative.

Element ∆egH(kJ/mol)
F –333
S –200
Br –325
Ar +96

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Consider the following elements.

Which of the following is/are true about A', B', C' and D' ?

A. Order of atomic radii: B'<A'<D'<C'

B. Order of metallic character : B'<A'<D'<C'

C. Size of the element : D'<C'<B'<A'

D. Order of ionic radii : B'+<A'+<D'+<C'+

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
In a group the atomic size increases due to the addition of shells as we move from one period to another. Across a period the atomic size decreases as the number of shells remain the same while the nuclear charge increases. This leads to the pulling of electrons from the outermost shell towards the nucleus thereby decreasing the size.

 Metallic character increases as we move down the group because the atomic size increases which lead to easy loss of electrons. On the other hand, it decreases across a period as we move from left to right. This happens because there is an increase in nuclear charge which makes it difficult for an atom to lose electrons.

In general along the period from left to right, size decreases and metallic character decreases.

In general down the group, size increases and metallic character increases.

B'<A' (size) C'>A' (size)

D'<C' (size) D'>B' (size)

B'<A' (metallic character)

D'<C' (metallic character)

B'+<A'+ (size)

D'+<C'+ (size)

∴ C statement is incorrect.

Hence ,option B is the answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement (I) : Both metal and non-metal exist in $\mathrm{p}$ and d-block elements.
Statement (II) : Non-metals have higher ionisation enthalpy and higher electronegativity than the metals.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the option given below:
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
questions.solution:
I. In p-Block both metals and non metals are present but in d-Block only metals are present

p-block elements: The elements which are present in group 13-18. The elements in p-blocks are of all kind metals and non-metals.
Transition elements: Those elements which are in groups from three to twelve. They are called a transition because they are in between the s- block elements and p-block elements. For example, scandium, iron, zinc, etc. Transition elements are also known as d-block elements.

II. EN and IE of non-metals are greater than those of metals.

Ionization Energy: The amount of energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom is known as ionization energy.

  • The size of non-metals is small as compared to metals due to which electrons are more tightly held in non-metals.
  • Non-Metals are electronegative elements and tend to share or accept electrons in a chemical reaction.
  • Due to the electrons accepting nature of non-metals, they gains more electrons to complete their octet.
  • So, non-metals have high ionization enthalpy due to their small size and high electronegativity as compared to metal.

I - False, II-True

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Number of complexes from the following with even number of unpaired " \(\mathrm{d}\) " electrons is \(\qquad\) \(\left[\mathrm{V}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+},\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+}\)
[Given atomic numbers : \(\mathrm{V}=23, \mathrm{Cr}=24, \mathrm{Fe}=26, \mathrm{Ni}=28 \mathrm{Cu}=29\)]
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & {\left[\mathrm{V}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+} \rightarrow \mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3} \\ & { }_{23} \mathrm{~V}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^3 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 \\ & \left.\mathrm{~V}^{+3}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^2, \mathrm{n}=2 \text { (even number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right) \\ & {\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & { }_{24} \mathrm{Cr}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 4 \mathrm{~s}^1 \\ & \left.\mathrm{Cr}^{+2}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^4, \mathrm{n}=4 \text { (even number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & {\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^5 4 \mathrm{~s}^0 \\ & \mathrm{n}=5\left(\text { odd number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right) \\ & {\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & \mathrm{Ni}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^8 4 \mathrm{~s}^2 \\ & \left.\mathrm{Ni}^{+3}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^7, \mathrm{n}=3 \text { (odd number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right) \\ & {\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{2+} \rightarrow \mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2} \\ & \mathrm{Cu}:-[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^9 4 \mathrm{~s}^0 \\ & \left.\mathrm{n}=1 \text { (odd number of unpaired } \mathrm{e}^{-}\right)\end{aligned}$

questions.q:
Match List - I with List - II.
\(\begin{array}{ll}
\text{List - I} & \text{List - II} \\
\text{Reaction} & \text{Type of redox reaction} \\
\text{(A) } \mathrm{TiCl}_4 & \text{(I) } \mathrm{e}^2, \mathrm{t}_2^0 \\
\text{(B) } \left[\mathrm{FeO}_4\right]^{2-} & \text{(II) } e^4, t_2^3 \\
\text{(C) } \left[\mathrm{FeCl}_4\right]^{-} & \text{(III) } e^0, t_2^0 \\
\text{(D) } \left[\mathrm{CoCl}_4\right]^{2-} & \text{(IV) } \mathrm{e}^2, \mathrm{t}_2^3
\end{array}\)
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \text { (A) }\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+} \\ & \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}: 3 \mathrm{~d}^3 \\ & \mathrm{n}=3 \text { (unpaired electrons) } \\ & \mu \simeq 3.87 \text { B.M. (II) } \\ & \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { (B) }\left[\mathrm{NiCl}_4\right]^{2-} \\ & \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}: 3 \mathrm{~d}^8 \\ & \mathrm{n}=2 \\ & \mu \simeq 2.83 \text { B.M. (IV) } \\ & \end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { (C) }\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{3-} \\ & \mathrm{Co}^{3+}: 3 \mathrm{~d}^6 \\ & \mathrm{n}=4 \\ & \mu \simeq 4.90 \text { B.M. (I) }\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { (D) }\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_4\right]^{2-} \\ & \mathrm{Ni}^{2+}: 3 \mathrm{~d}^8 \\ & \mathrm{n}=0 \\ & \mu=0 \text { B.M. (III) } \\ & \end{aligned}$
SECTION -B

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The Spin only magnetic moment value of square planar complex PtNH32ClNH2CH3Cl is _______ B.M. (Nearest integer)
( Given atomic number for Pt=78)
questions.solution:
Spin only magnetic moment can be calculated by the following formula
Magnetic moment =n(n+2)
Where, n is number of unpaired electrons.

In the given compound, Pt2+d8

Pt2+→dsp2 hybridization and have no unpaired electrons..

∴ Magnetic moment

 =n(n+2)=0(0+2)=0

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The correct statements from the following are:

A. The strength of anionic ligands can be explained by crystal field theory.

B. Valence bond theory does not give a quantitative interpretation of kinetic stability of coordination compounds.

C. The hybridization involved in formation of [Ni(CN)4] 2– complex is dsp2.

D. The number of possible isomer(s) of cis-[PtCl2 (en)2] 2+ is one

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
The strength of ligands discussed in spectrochemical series.

According to the limitations of VBT,

It does not give a quantitative interpretation of the thermodynamic or kinetic stabilities of coordination compounds.

 Hybridisation of [Ni(CN)4] -2 is dsp2.

The outer electronic configuration of Ni2+ ion is 3d84s0.

It is a square planar complex. Cyanide ion is a strong field ligand, thus all electrons will be paired.

One 3d, one 4s and two 4p orbitals undergo dsp2 hybridization, four equivalent dsp2 hybrid orbitals which are occupied by 4 electron pairs of 4 cyanide ligands.

This forms [Ni(CN)4] -2

cis-[PtCl2 (en)2] 2+ complex exhibits optical isomerism, hence, it has two isomers.

Hence statement B and C are correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Select the option with correct property:
questions.solution:
Properties of the diamagnetic materials are: The diamagnetic materials have all the paired electrons, and none of the electrons is the valence, resulting in the absence of atomic dipoles in these materials. This happens because the overall magnetic moment of each atom in the compound cancels out.

NiCO4→ diamagnetic, sp3 hybridization, number of unpaired electrons =0

If the substance contains unpaired electrons, it is a paramagnetic substance.

NiCl42−, → paramagnetic, sp3 hybridization, number of unpaired electrons =2

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List I with List II

  LIST – I
(Complex ion)		   LIST – II
(Electronic
Configuration
A. CrH2O63+ I. t2g2eg0
B. FeH2O63+ II. t2g3eg0
C. NiH2O62+ III. t2g3eg2
D. VH2O63+ IV. t2g6eg2

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
The t2g (triplet, lower energy) orbitals are the dxy, dxz and dyz orbitals. The eg (doublet, higher energy) orbitals are the dz2 and dx2-y2 orbitals. These split d-orbitals are called t2g and egbecause they are split by the effect of the surrounding ligand ions in a crystal field.

CrH2O63+ Contains Cr3+:Ar3d3:t2g3ego
FeH2O63+ Contains Fe3+:Ar3d5:t2g3eg2
NiH2O62+ Contains Ni2+:Ar3d8:t2g6eg2

VH2O63+ Contains V3+:Ar3d2:t2g2ego

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mi>Co</mi><msub><mfenced><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>6</mn></msub></mrow></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><msub><mi>CoF</mi><mn>6</mn></msub></mfenced><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> are respectively known as:
options: Spin free Complex, Spin paired Complex
Spin paired Complex, Spin free Complex
Outer orbital Complex, Inner orbital Complex
Inner orbital Complex, Spin paired Complex
questions.solution:
In the complex CoNH363+ Ammonia is strong field ligand and electronic configuration of 

Co3+⇒3 d6t2 g6,eg0

Hybridisation: d2sp3

Pairing of electrons takes place due to the strong field ligand. Hence, it is inner orbital complex (spin paired complex).

In the complex CoF63- fluoride is weak field ligand and electronic configuration of  Co3+ ⇒3d6t2g4,eg2.

Hybridisation: sp3d2

Pairing of electrons not takes place due to the weak field ligand. Hence, it is outer orbital complex (spin free complex).

Hence option B is the answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In which one of the following metal carbonyls, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>CO</mi></math> forms a bridge between metal atoms?
questions.solution: In the most common bridging mode, denoted μ2 or simply μ, the CO  ligand bridges a pair of metals. This bonding mode is observed in the commonly available metal carbonyls: Co2CO8, Fe2CO9, Fe3CO12and Co4CO12. In certain higher nuclearity clusters, CO bridges between three or even four metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: When \(\mathrm{MnO}_2\) and \(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4\) is added to a salt (A), the greenish yellow gas liberated as salt (A) is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 2 \mathrm{NH}_4 \mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{MnO}_2+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{MnSO}_4 \\ & +\left(\mathrm{NH}_4\right)_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\underset{\substack{\text { greenish } \\ \text { yellow } \\ \text { solution }}}{\mathrm{Cl}_2 \uparrow}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The metal that shows highest and maximum number of oxidation state is :
questions.solution: Mn shows highest oxidation state $\left(\mathrm{Mn}^{+7}\right)$ in $3 \mathrm{~d}$ series metals.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The spin-only magnetic moment value of the ion among \(\mathrm{Ti}^{2+}, \mathrm{V}^{2+}, \mathrm{Co}^{3+}\) and \(\mathrm{Cr}^{2+}\), that acts as strong oxidising agent in aqueous solution is _______ BM (Near integer).
(Given atomic numbers : \(\mathrm{Ti}: 22, \mathrm{~V}: 23, \mathrm{Cr}: 24, \mathrm{Co}: 27\))
questions.solution:
Strong oxidising agent $=\mathrm{Co}^{+3}$
No. of unpaired $\mathrm{e}^{-}$in $\mathrm{Co}^{+3}\left[3 \mathrm{~d}^6\right]=4$
Hence $\mu=\sqrt{\mathrm{n}(\mathrm{n}+2)}=\sqrt{24} \mathrm{BM}$
Nearest integer $=5$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The 'spin only' magnetic moment value of \(\mathrm{MO}_4{ }^{2-}\) is _______ BM. (Where M is a metal having least metallic radii. among \(\mathrm{Sc}, \mathrm{Ti}, \mathrm{V}, \mathrm{Cr}, \mathrm{Mn}\) and \(\mathrm{Zn}\) ).
(Given atomic number: \(\mathrm{Sc}=21, \mathrm{Ti}=22, \mathrm{~V}=23, \mathrm{Cr}=24, \mathrm{Mn}=25\) and \(\mathrm{Zn}=30\))
questions.solution:
Metal having least metallic radii among $\mathrm{Sc}, \mathrm{Ti}, \mathrm{V}$, $\mathrm{Cr}, \mathrm{Mn} \& \mathrm{Zn}$ is $\mathrm{Cr}$.
Spin only magnetic moment of $\mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}$.
Here $\mathrm{Cr}^{+6}$ is in $\mathrm{d}^0$ configuration (diamagnetic).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Number of colourless lanthanoid ions among the following is ________
\(\mathrm{Eu}^{3+}, \mathrm{Lu}^{3+}, \mathrm{Nd}^{3+}, \mathrm{La}^{3+}, \mathrm{Sm}^{3+}\)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{La}^{+3}-[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^0 \\
& \mathrm{Nd}^{+3}-[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^3 \\
& \mathrm{Sm}^{+3}-[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^5 \\
& \mathrm{Eu}^{+3}-[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^6 \\
& \mathrm{Lu}^{+3}-[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^{14}
\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{La}^{+3}$ and $\mathrm{Lu}^{+3}$ do not show any colour because no unpaired electron is present.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Which of the following acts as a strong reducing agent? (Atomic number : <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ce</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>58</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>Eu</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>63</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Gd</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>64</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>Lu</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>71</mn><mo>)</mo></math>
questions.solution:
A reducing agent is one of the reactants of an oxidation-reduction reaction which reduces the other reactant by giving out electrons to the reactant. If the reducing agent does not pass electrons to other substances in a reaction, then the reduction process cannot occur.

Europium (atomic number 63) has the electronic configuration Xe4f76s2. It loses two electrons from the 6s energy level and attains a highly stable, half-filled 4f7 configuration, and hence it readily forms Eu2+ion. Eu2+ then changes to the common oxidation states of lanthanides +3 and forms Eu3+, acting as a strong reducing agent.

Eu+2→Eu+3+1e-[Xe]4f76 s0[Xe]4f66 s0

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II

  List I (Species)   List II (Electronic distribution)
A. Cr+2 i. 3d8
B. Mn+ ii. 3d34s1
C. Ni+2 iii. .3d4
D. V+ iv. 3d54s1

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Electrons must be removed from the outermost orbital to form an ion.

The electronic configurations of the elements are as follows,

Cr24→[Ar]3 d54s1; Cr2+→[Ar]3d4

Mn25→[Ar]3d54s2;Mn+→[Ar]3d5 4s1

Ni28→[Ar]3d84s2; Ni2+→[Ar]3d8

 23V→[Ar] 3d34s2; V+→[Ar]3d34s1

Hence option B is the answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Which of the following compounds show colour due to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi></math> transition?
questions.solution:
The hydrated copper sulphate 

CuSO4.5H2O is blue in color. The ligand (water molecules) cause splitting of d – orbitals. We know that, due to this, the process of d – d transition and color transition is facilitated.

Whereas, on the other hand the anhydrous copper sulphate CuSO4
 is colourless. In the absence of ligand (water molecules), splitting of d orbitals is not possible. Hence, d – d transition is not possible.
We can understand the above from the following statements.

CuSO4.5H2O is blue in colour because its oxidation state +2, thus it has unpaired electrons in it. So, d –d transition is possible so it exhibits colour where on the other hand the anhydrous copper sulphate complex, d – d transition is not possible that’s why it is colourless.

CuSO4.5H2O

Cu2+:3 d94 s0

unpaired electron present so it show colour due to d-d transition.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : In aqueous solutions Cr2+ is reducing while Mn3+ is oxidising in nature.

Reason R: Extra stability to half filled electronic configuration is observed than incompletely filled electronic configuration.

In the light of the above statement, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
options: Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> are true and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="italic">R</mi></mfenced></math> is the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math>

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mfenced></math> is not the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></mfenced></math>
questions.solution:
A reducing agent, or reductant, loses electrons and is oxidized in a chemical reaction. A reducing agent is typically in one of its lower possible oxidation states, and is known as the electron donor.

Cr2+ is reducing as it configuration changes from d4 to d3 due to formation of Cr3+, which has half filled t2 g level, on other hand, the change Mn3+ to Mn2+ result half filled d5configuration which has extra stability.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In alkaline medium. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mi>MnO</mi><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo></msubsup></math> oxidises <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi><mo>-</mo></msup></math> to
questions.solution:
In neutral or faintly alkaline solution the oxidation of iodide to iodate taken place with potassium permanganate.

2MnO4-+H2O+I-→2MnO2+2OH-+IO3-In the above reaction MnO4- is an oxidising agent and I- is a reducing agent. 

Hence option D is the answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Choose the correct statements from the following
A. Mn2O7 is an oil at room temperature  B. V2O4 reacts with acid to give VO22+
C. CrO is a basic oxide  D. V2O5 does not react with acid
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
All the metals except scandium form MO oxides, which are ionic. Mn2O7 is a covalent green oil.  In vanadium there is gradual change from the basic V2O3 to less basic V2O4 and to amphoteric V2O5 . V2O4 dissolves in acids to give VO2+ salts. Similarly, V2O5 reacts with alkalies as well as acids to give VO43- and VO4+ respectively. The well characterised CrO is basic but Cr2O3 is amphoteric.

(A) Mn2O7 is green oil at room temperature.

(B) V2O4 dissolve in acids to give VO2+ salts.

(C) CrO is basic oxide.

Hence option B is the answer,

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The correct statements from the following are :
(A) The decreasing order of atomic radii of group 13 elements is \(\mathrm{Tl}>\) In \(>\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{Al}>\mathrm{B}\).
(B) Down the group 13 electronegativity decreases from top to bottom.
(C) \(\mathrm{Al}\) dissolves in dil. \(\mathrm{HCl}\) and liberates \(\mathrm{H}_2\) but conc. \(\mathrm{HNO}_3\) renders \(\mathrm{Al}\) passive by forming a protective oxide layer on the surface.
(D) All elements of group 13 exhibits highly stable +1 oxidation state.
(E) Hybridisation of \(\mathrm{Al}\) in \(\left[\mathrm{Al}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+}\) ion is \(\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2\).
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
questions.solution:
A. size order $\mathrm{T} \ell>\mathrm{In}>\mathrm{Al}>\mathrm{Ga}>\mathrm{B}$ B. Electronegativity order $\mathrm{B}>\mathrm{Al} < \mathrm{Ga} < \mathrm{In} < \mathrm{T} \ell$ D. $\mathrm{B}, \mathrm{Al}$ are more stable in +3 oxidation state
So, only C, E statements are correct.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The number of ions from the following that are expected to behave as oxidising agent is :
\(\mathrm{Sn}^{4+}, \mathrm{Sn}^{2+}, \mathrm{Pb}^{2+}, \mathrm{Tl}^{3+}, \mathrm{Pb}^{4+}, \mathrm{Tl}^{+}\)
questions.solution: Due to inert pair effect; $\mathrm{T} \ell^{+3}$ and $\mathrm{Pb}^{+4}$ can behave as oxidising agents.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Give below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion \(\mathrm{A}\) and the other is labelled as Reason R:
Assertion A: The stability order of +1 oxidation state of Ga, In and T1 is \(\mathrm{Ga}  < \mathrm{In} < \) T1.
Reason R: The inert pair effect stabilizes the lower oxidation state down the group.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both \(\mathrm{A}\) and \(\mathrm{R}\) are true and \(\mathrm{R}\) is the correct explanation of \(\mathrm{A}\).
Both \(\mathrm{A}\) and \(\mathrm{R}\) are true but \(\mathrm{R}\) is NOT the correct explanation of \(\mathrm{A}\).
questions.solution:
The relative stability of +1 oxidation state progressively increases for heavier elements due to inert pair effect.
$\therefore$ Stability of $\mathrm{A} \ell^{+1} < \mathrm{Ga}^{+1} < \mathrm{In}^{+1} < \mathrm{T} \ell^{+1}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A) : Melting point of Boron (2453 K) is unusually high in group 13 elements.
Reason (R) : Solid Boron has very strong crystalline lattice.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below ;
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) Is not the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
questions.solution:
Boron is non-metallic in nature. It is extremely hard and black coloured solid. Due to a very strong crystalline lattice, boron has an unusually high melting point. The rest of the members are soft metals with a low melting point and high electrical conductivity.

Hence, Solid Boron has very strong crystalline lattice, so its melting point is unusually high in group 13 elements.

Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List - I with List - II.
List - I Compound List-II Use
(A) Carbon tetrachloride (I) Paint remover
(B) Methylene chloride (II) Refrigerators and air conditioners
(C) DDT (III) Fire extinguisher
(D) Freons (IV) Non Biodegradable insecticide
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
CCl4 is used as fire extinguishers under the name of pyrene.
The dense vapours of carbon tetrachloride forms a protective layer on the burning objects and avoids the oxygen or air to come in contact with the fire from the burning objects and provides incombustible vapours.

Chemical paint strippers that include methylene chloride and phenol have been extensively used to remove polymer coatings from metallic substrates. These strippers are inexpensive and remove polymeric organic coatings quickly and easily from a variety of metallic substrates without damage to the substrate.

Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane (DDT) is an organic halogen that acts by killing insects rapidly upon contact. DDT is harm to all biota of the planet as it is non-biodegradable, and tends to become more concentrated as it moves up the food chain.

The freons are simple fluorinated and aliphatic organic compounds that contain fluorine, carbon, hydrogen, chlorine, bromine etc. These are used as the refrigerants and propellants. Chlorofluorocarbons, Hydrochlorofluorocarbons are the types of freons. They have a low boiling point and low suface tension due to which they are used in refrigerators, air conditioners etc. However, they undergo photo-dissociation when exposed to the UV rays, releasing a reactive chlorine ion that is capable of reacting with ozone. This reaction is responsible for the depletion of the ozone in the stratosphere. Hence, the correct answer is 'Freon'.

Hence, CCl4 used in fire extinguisher. CH2Cl2 used as paint remover. Freons used in refrigerator and AC.DDT used as non Biodegradable insecticide.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements :

Statement I: Group 13 trivalent halides get easily hydrolysed by water due to their covalent nature.

Statement II: AlCl3 upon hydrolysis in acidified aqueous solution forms octahedral AlH2O63+ ion.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: Statement I is true but statement II is false
Statement I is false but statement II is true
Both statement I and statement II are false
Both statement I and statement II are true
questions.solution:
In trivalent state most of the 13th group compounds being covalent are hydrolysed in water. Trichlorides of group 13 on hydrolysis in water form tetrahedral MOH4− species, the hybridisation state of element M is sp3.

In case of aluminium, acidified aqueous solution forms octahedral AlH2O63+ ion, which has an aluminium as a central metal atom and water molecules as ligands. The aluminium is in +3  oxidation state and forms 6 sp3d2 hybrid orbitals. 

Hence option D is the answer.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The strongest reducing agent amont the following is:
questions.solution: The reducing character of the hydrides of Group 15 elements increases from NH3 to BiH3 (Bismuthine) because the reducing character depends upon the stability of the hydride.The greater the unstability of hydride,the greater is the its reducing character. Since,the BiH3 is the least stable (because the size of central atom is greatest & therefore its tendency to form stable covalent bond with small hydrogen atom decreases,as a result the bond strength decreases) in this series,hence the reducing character increases.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Anomalous behaviour of oxygen is due to its
questions.solution:
Due to its small size, Oxygen exhibits high electronegativity, a high charge/ radius ratio, and no d-orbital oxygen shows anomalous behaviour.

Oxygen exists as a diatomic molecule as O2 comprising a double bond whereas all the other elements of group 16 exist as polyatomic molecules.

Oxygen is paramagnetic and exists in a gaseous state at room temperature, which is completely opposite of the other Group 16 elements.

The small size and high electronegativity of Oxygen enable it in the formation of pπ-pπ multiple bonds.
